surface_verification.justification: No startable user-facing surface: WP2 hardens the orchestrator's shipwright_run_config.json persistence layer (atomic writes + path-coordinated advisory lock) and a Stop hook timeout. No API/UI/store/message contract changed. Behavior is empirically driven through real OS processes by test_runconfig_concurrency.py (3 writer families) and the full pytest suite.

behaviors.behavior: F11: save_run_config writes atomically (tmp+os.replace) — no torn read
F11: update_step reloads a FRESH config under the lock and does not clobber a concurrent writer's fields
F11: run_config_lock is mutually exclusive and coordinates by path with phase_task_lifecycle._PhaseTasksLock
F12: phase_task_lifecycle._write_config is atomic — no half-written config under concurrency
behaviors.evidence: test_phase_task_lifecycle.py suite (36) PASSED; concurrency test parses final config as valid JSON

behaviors.behavior: F13: Stop-fallback outer update-step timeout strictly exceeds the inner compliance timeout
behaviors.evidence: test_runconfig_timeout_invariant.py::test_phase_completion_outer_timeout_exceeds_compliance_inner PASSED (captures actual 60 > 30 + asserts the update-step command)

behaviors.behavior: file_lock._acquire_windows seek(0) keeps byte-0 lock coordination deterministic regardless of lock-file content
behaviors.evidence: test_run_config_concurrent_writers.py (2 slow) + test_retention_race.py (4) PASSED; coordinate test PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Integration: orchestrator + phase_task + phase_history writer families compose under true multi-process concurrency with no truncation or lost update
update_step behavior parity (bootstrap/standalone, ask-issues early return, inform notes, split-loop, in_progress/failed, returned config)
